title: Sikandar Bagh
tagline: Sikandar Bagh — heritage city of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Sikandar Bagh (Hindi: सिकन्दर बाग़, Urdu: سِکندر باغ), formerly known by the British as Sikunder/Sikandra/Secundra Bagh, is a villa and garden enclosed by a fortified wall, with lo
overview.description: Sikandar Bagh (Hindi: सिकन्दर बाग़, Urdu: سِکندر باغ), formerly known by the British as Sikunder/Sikandra/Secundra Bagh, is a villa and garden enclosed by a fortified wall, with loopholes, gateway and corner bastions, approx. 150 yards square, c. 4.5 acres (1.8 ha), located in the city of Lucknow, Oudh, Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by the last Nawab of Oudh, Wajid Ali Shah (1822–1887), as a summer residence.

nearestAirport.name: Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport, Lucknow

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: The Residency, Lucknow
topPlaces.description: The Residency, also called as the British Residency and Residency Complex, is a group of several buildings in a common precinct in the city of Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India. It served as the residence for the British Resident General who was a representative in the court of the Nawab of Awadh or Oudh.

topPlaces.name: Iron Bridge, Lucknow
topPlaces.description: The Iron Bridge over the Gomti River in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, was one of the earliest iron bridges in India, erected in 1845. Originally designed by British civil engineer John Rennie, the cast-iron sections were manufactured by Butterley Company of Derbyshire, England, between 1812 and 1814, and shipped to Calcutta, finally arriving in Lucknow in 1816.

topPlaces.name: Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama
topPlaces.description: Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama is an Islamic seminary in Lucknow, India, founded in 1898 by Nadwatul Ulama under the leadership of Muhammad Ali Mungeri. Its purpose was to produce scholars who could integrate the traditional Islamic learning of Darul Uloom Deoband with the modern educational approach of Aligarh Muslim University.

topPlaces.name: Rumi Darwaza
topPlaces.description: The Rumi Darwaza (sometimes known as the Turkish Gate), in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India, is a gateway which was built by Nawab Asaf-Ud-Daula in 1784. It is an example of Awadhi architecture.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Lucknow
topPlaces.description: The siege of Lucknow was the prolonged defence of the British Residency within the city of Lucknow from rebel sepoys (Indian soldiers in the British East India Company's Army) during the Indian Rebellion of 1857. After two successive relief attempts had reached the city, the defenders and civilians were evacuated from the Residency, which was then abandoned.

topPlaces.name: Bhatkhande Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Bhatkhande Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya (BSV), formerly Bhatkhande Music Institute Deemed University (2000-2022), Bhatkhande College of Hindustani Music (1966-2000) and Marris College of Music (1926-1966), is a state university in Lucknow. Co-founded in 1926 by Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande, it was declared a deemed university by University Grants Commission (UGC) in 2000, and upgraded into a state university in 2022 by The Bhatkhande Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya Act, 2022.

topPlaces.name: Lal Baradari
topPlaces.description: Lal Baradari (لال برادری) is a historic 19th‑century building located within the campus of University of Lucknow in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India. The structure forms part of the former royal gardens of the Awadh Nawabs and is considered a heritage monument of the Nawabi era.

topPlaces.name: Amir-ud-daula Public Library
topPlaces.description: The Amir-ud-daula Public Library (Hindi: अमीर-उद-दौला पब्लिक लाइब्रेरी, Urdu: امر-اد-دلا پبلک لائبریری), is a public library in Lucknow, India.

items.note: The Residency, also called as the British Residency and Residency Complex, is a group of several buildings in a common precinct in the city of Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India. It served as the residence for the British Resident General who was a representative in the court of the Nawab of Awadh or Oudh.

The Iron Bridge over the Gomti River in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, was one of the earliest iron bridges in India, erected in 1845. Originally designed by British civil engineer John Rennie, the cast-iron sections were manufactured by Butterley Company of Derbyshire, England, between 1812 and 1814, and shipped to Calcutta, finally arriving in Lucknow in 1816.

Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama is an Islamic seminary in Lucknow, India, founded in 1898 by Nadwatul Ulama under the leadership of Muhammad Ali Mungeri. Its purpose was to produce scholars who could integrate the traditional Islamic learning of Darul Uloom Deoband with the modern educational approach of Aligarh Muslim University.
The Rumi Darwaza (sometimes known as the Turkish Gate), in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India, is a gateway which was built by Nawab Asaf-Ud-Daula in 1784. It is an example of Awadhi architecture.

The siege of Lucknow was the prolonged defence of the British Residency within the city of Lucknow from rebel sepoys (Indian soldiers in the British East India Company's Army) during the Indian Rebellion of 1857. After two successive relief attempts had reached the city, the defenders and civilians were evacuated from the Residency, which was then abandoned.

Bhatkhande Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya (BSV), formerly Bhatkhande Music Institute Deemed University (2000-2022), Bhatkhande College of Hindustani Music (1966-2000) and Marris College of Music (1926-1966), is a state university in Lucknow. Co-founded in 1926 by Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande, it was declared a deemed university by University Grants Commission (UGC) in 2000, and upgraded into a state university in 2022 by The Bhatkhande Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya Act, 2022.

Lal Baradari (لال برادری) is a historic 19th‑century building located within the campus of University of Lucknow in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India. The structure forms part of the former royal gardens of the Awadh Nawabs and is considered a heritage monument of the Nawabi era.
The Amir-ud-daula Public Library (Hindi: अमीर-उद-दौला पब्लिक लाइब्रेरी, Urdu: امر-اد-دلا پبلک لائبریری), is a public library in Lucknow, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sikandar Bagh?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sikandar Bagh is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sikandar Bagh?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport, Lucknow (12 km) and the nearest railway station is Lucknow Charbagh (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sikandar Bagh?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sikandar Bagh at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sikandar Bagh?
faq.a: Stays in Sikandar Bagh start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sikandar Bagh?
faq.a: The top attractions are The Residency, Lucknow, Iron Bridge, Lucknow, Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama, Rumi Darwaza, Siege of Lucknow.
